## [00:00 - 03:06]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the replay promo part of your webinar sales funnel. So if let's go back and just put you in place of where this happens in the in the system. So you're mailing to your list or you've got external traffic coming in or you got affiliate emails coming into a webinar registration page. From there, they go they get an audience promo. They attend the webinar. If they don't didn't buy they then see this replay promo where we're promoting people to watch the replay of the webinar. They come from your list, but in other words, there's multiple ways where we get them to this replay promo. If it's external traffic or affiliate email, we're getting them in, we're injecting them in. If they say no to the webinar registration, we're re inject injecting them into the campaign at the replay promo stage. So this is a very important stage because it determines everything that happens after that. So your mission here is to get them to watch the recording of the webinar. And if not, you want them to at least be still open to considering your offer. So in other words, you don't want to burn bridges at this point. You still want them to be very positive about your offer because again, just like there's 70% of the people won't watch won't even attend a webinar. There's about 80% of the people that won't ever watch the replay of the webinar. So we still don't want to tick them off, but we do want to get that 20% who will because every little bit helps. So here's how the replay promo works. Basically, this is two to four emails inviting them to watch the replay of your webinar. Let me take you through each one of those emails. Email one is basically watch the video. Watch the video. Watch the video of the replay. You're sending this as soon as possible after the webinar. So if you are using a service that automatically records and encodes your webinar, you can usually within an hour after the webinar have a recording of that video of that webinar be able to slap it up on a page. I'll talk about what that page should look like in a separate video and be able to email that out. Personally, I write that email before I ever hold the webinar so that everything's ready. All I had to do is once I've got that email, once I've got that video on the landing page, then I just press send and send this out. And the focus here is that you're talking about something that you talked about in the webinar.

## [03:06 - 06:13]

So it's something amazing that you taught in that webinar that they're definitely going to want to see. And it also includes what else are going to learn from watching the replay. And most of the cases you're talking, basically you're talking about the stuff you marketed, used to mark the webinar in the first place. Now you're saying, Hey, it's all done. You can go watch it. But I like to have this be at least slightly different. I like to point out something new that they haven't seen before so that they'll be willing to come and watch the replay. Email number two is what I call timestamps from the webinar. So this is sent the next morning after the webinar. And it contains three key points from the webinar, including the timestamps. So what you're doing is you're saying, Hey, I taught this really cool thing at five minutes and 25 seconds into the webinar. Go here to watch the webinar. And I'll put three of those into that. So three different things that I taught with three different timestamps so that they can look at it and say, Well, I'm not interested in that one, but I'm very interested in that one. They click on it, go to 13 minutes and 26 seconds and watch that portion of the webinar. And what oftentimes happens is if I can get them to go in and watch at 13 minutes and 26 seconds, they're going to watch a whole lot more than just that point. They might go back to the beginning. They might watch from that point all the way to the end. Whatever works, I'll take it. So I'm sending. So the key point of this is that you're putting in these timestamps to show people, Hey, you can skip all the other stuff if you want to and just go to these parts and get huge value. ## [06:13 - 09:17]

So it all depends on whether you're trying to on how tight your cart open period is. But you're either sending this the afternoon of the day after the webinar or the next morning after that. Well, remember this chart with the showing the external traffic and the affiliate email and the opt in delivery down here? So what you're saying in the email to them is, Hey, thanks for attending multiple people asked for the recording. So here it is. Then there is the group who attended, but they left before the offer. So here you're saying thanks for attending. Hey, I noticed you had to leave before the end. And so here's the recording and here's some things that you may have missed. If you do it without getting creepy, you can pull that one off. All right, the third group is the group who registered, but they didn't attend. So here I'm saying, sorry, you missed the webinar. Here's what you missed. Here's a copy of the other recording. The fourth group is the people who didn't register. So I recently recorded a webinar about the subject of your webinar. Here's what you missed. And here's a copy of the recording. So what you can see here is that much of this is copy and paste. Or even if they attended but left before the end of it. And so you're writing slightly different emails for each one of these groups. Now you may say this sounds hard. Well, guess what? As I said, it's a cut and paste. And so it's literally about two minutes of customizing each of those four emails. It's not hard. You can do it in a very short period of time. Bonus tip. None of these emails in the replay promo mention what you're selling. They don't mention the offer at all. Everything you're doing at this point is pushing them to watch the replay of the webinar. So in other words, at the moment, everything's about here's this free stuff I gave you. Come watch it. Now, one other side note. If your webinar converted at less than 20 to 25%, you may want to only send two of these emails.

## [12:17 - 13:12]

So I'm only going to send four emails out about the replay. If my replay really did its job and did a good job of converting the people who are on the webinar itself. So that's the replay. Basically, you're going through a series of four, two to four emails where you are sending people to the replay to the recording of the webinar. Now, one other side note. There are some webinar packages that basically enable you to recreate the feeling of a webinar. Those are designed for an evergreen webinar kind of setup. That's not what you send these people to. In this case, you're sending them to an actual recording of the webinar without the comments streaming in and everything else like that. It's just a recording of the webinar, a classic webinar replay.
